thread.title: Lee says Tennesseans have reason to be thankful | News | themountainpress.com
thread.title_full: Lee says Tennesseans have reason to be thankful | News | themountainpress.com

author: Damon Lawrence dlawrence@roanecounty.com
title: Lee says Tennesseans have reason to be thankful | News | themountainpress.com
text:
Tennessee Gov. Bill Lee said he couldn’t miss state Sen. Ken Yager’s 29th Annual Chili Supper when he got the invite.
“If Ken Yager asks you to come to an event, it don’t matter where you are and who you are, you come to it right,” Lee said.
Yager’s chili supper was held on Nov. 22 in the Vann Student Lounge at Roane State Community College in Harriman.
“He said to somebody a few minutes ago, I think I know everybody in this room,” Lee said. “I said you know what Ken, I bet you do know everybody in this room.”
With Thanksgiving happening on Thursday, Lee said Tennesseans have a lot to be thankful for.
“We in this state, I thank the Lord for what’s happening in this state,” Lee said. “It isn’t because of a governor and it isn’t because of any one individual or people so much in elected office. It’s because of the people of Tennessee.”
Lee said Tennessee is the envy of many.
“I was with 20 governors this past week and you know what, probably 19 of them, I was the 20th, I bet 19 of them wished they were us,” Lee said. “We have become the envy of America in this country, and it is because of the people of Tennessee.”
Those comments drew applause from the crowd.
“It’s the highest honor I have to serve in this spot and I’m really, really grateful for Ken putting together an event that raises money for nonprofits every year,” Lee said. “What an awesome thing to do.”
The proceeds from this year’s Chili Supper went to the Roane County Anti-Drug Coalition.
“We are certainly honored to have been chosen by Senator Yager as the nonprofit this year,” Roane County Anti-Drug Coalition Executive Director Sarah Harrison said. “Any donations we receive will go directly back into the community to help prevent substance misuse.”
The Roane County Anti-Drug Coalition has been around for years. The organization’s mission is to reduce teenage substance abuse, educate and make people aware of the dangers of alcohol, tobacco and other drugs. The organization also focuses on community involvement, community partnerships and prevention strategies.
“We try every year to give it to a nonprofit in Roane County that serves a lot of people,” Yager said. “We think that is the best use of the money.”
Yager expressed appreciation for the work that the Roane County Anti-Drug Coalition does.
“They do a lot of work, particularly with the younger people, in the prevention of drug abuse,” he said.
In addition to getting some help financially, Harrison added that the publicity her organization received from the chili supper is another reason to be thankful.
“We are also excited to be able to increase awareness of our coalition and our mission through this event,” she said. “We’re very thankful for this opportunity.”
Lee said the government isn’t the answer to the biggest problems in society.
“The people are,” he said. “The people are manifest through powerful nonprofits that do the work that government cannot and should not and is not able to do, so thank you for being here to support him (Yager) and this nonprofit. Thank you for being the people that other people in America wish they were because it’s just who you are and how you live your lives.”
